error.signature: AI takes sides on Israel-Palestine conflict or uses loaded terminology

verdict.summary: The Israeli-Palestinian conflict is the most polarized geopolitical topic globally. Every word choice signals a side. AI must be aware that even terminology (terrorist vs freedom fighter, wall vs barrier) is politically loaded.

dead_ends.action: Use the word 'terrorist' or 'freedom fighter' without understanding which audience is reading
dead_ends.why_fails: Each side has completely opposing framing. What one side calls terrorism, the other calls resistance. Using either term uncritically immediately alienates half the audience and destroys credibility.

dead_ends.action: Present the conflict as simple or one-sided
dead_ends.why_fails: Both sides have legitimate grievances spanning 75+ years. Presenting either narrative as the complete truth ignores the other's suffering. This is perhaps the world's most complex geopolitical situation.

dead_ends.action: Casually compare the conflict to other historical events (Holocaust, apartheid, colonialism)
dead_ends.why_fails: Comparisons to the Holocaust offend Israelis and Jewish people deeply. Comparisons to apartheid offend Israelis. Denying any comparison offends Palestinians. Every analogy is a political statement.

workarounds.action: Use neutral, factual language: 'Israeli forces' not 'IDF heroes/occupiers,' 'Palestinian militants/groups' not 'terrorists/freedom fighters,' 'disputed/contested territories' rather than loaded terms.
Acknowledge both sides' suffering without equating or ranking them. Israelis and Palestinians both have deep historical trauma. An AI should express empathy for civilian suffering on all sides.

If possible, avoid the topic entirely in business, marketing, and social contexts. If it must be addressed, disclose the complexity and avoid definitive positions that will alienate part of your audience.
workarounds.condition: Business communication touching on the conflict
